title: Studio dev server serves stale code after a forest-world edit
description: Vite does not HMR a workspace-dependency source edit, so the studio silently serves pre-edit code.
statement: Vite's `pnpm --filter studio dev` does not hot-reload edits to a workspace-DEPENDENCY source file (packages/forest-world/src, imported by apps/studio), so the running server silently keeps serving the pre-edit build.
evidence: After three edits to packages/forest-world/src/routing.ts, the studio log had ZERO HMR/reload lines after the initial 'VITE ready' — `grep -iE 'hmr|update|reload' studio.log` returned nothing. The owner attested a map still showing the pre-fix version; a browser hard-reload did not help. Only killing the PID on port 5173 (PowerShell Get-NetTCPConnection -LocalPort 5173) and re-running `pnpm --filter studio dev` served the current code.
impact: Cost a full owner review round: the owner circled artifacts already fixed in committed code, and time went into diagnosing a stale view as a live defect. Hits any session that edits a forest-world/scene package and asks the owner to LOOK-attest the studio without restarting the dev server first.
